root_cause.text: The `XSLTResult` class allowed the location of the XSLT stylesheet to be dynamically overridden by an untrusted HTTP request parameter (`xslt.location`), which could enable attackers to load and execute malicious stylesheets.
root_cause.evidence: The patch removes the code `String pathFromRequest = ServletActionContext.getRequest().getParameter("xslt.location");` and `if (pathFromRequest != null) path = pathFromRequest;` from the `getTemplates` method.

agent_logs.root_cause_candidate: The `XSLTResult` class allowed the location of the XSLT stylesheet to be dynamically overridden by an untrusted HTTP request parameter (`xslt.location`), which could enable attackers to load and execute malicious stylesheets.
agent_logs.evidence_points: The patch removes the code `String pathFromRequest = ServletActionContext.getRequest().getParameter("xslt.location");` and `if (pathFromRequest != null) path = pathFromRequest;` from the `getTemplates` method.

intent_record.intent_summary: Remove vulnerable xslt.location request parameter override to prevent arbitrary stylesheet loading.
intent_record.what: Removed the logic that overrides the stylesheet path with the 'xslt.location' request parameter and marked the path parameter as final.
intent_record.how: Prevents attackers from supplying an arbitrary stylesheet location via HTTP request parameters, eliminating a potential security vulnerability.

grouping_result.intent: Remove vulnerable xslt.location request parameter override to prevent arbitrary stylesheet loading.

relevance_review.reason: The group's core intent and changes directly remove the vulnerable 'xslt.location' request parameter override, which perfectly matches the vulnerability root cause.

candidates.rationale: This line retrieves the untrusted 'xslt.location' parameter from the request, which is the root cause of the vulnerability.
This line directly overrides the stylesheet path with the untrusted request parameter value, enabling the arbitrary stylesheet loading.

vuln_statements.explanation: This statement retrieves the untrusted 'xslt.location' parameter from the HTTP request, which enables the dynamic override of the XSLT stylesheet location.
This statement directly overrides the stylesheet path with the untrusted request parameter value, finalizing the malicious stylesheet location injection.

bic_trace_step.commit_msg:
Initial import of WebWork 2, version 2.2.2


git-svn-id: https://svn.apache.org/repos/asf/incubator/webwork2@388283 13f7
bic_trace_step.reason: The function `getTemplates` retrieves the untrusted HTTP request parameter 'xslt.location' on line 267 (`String pathFromRequest = ServletActionContext.getRequest().getParameter("xslt.location");`) and dynamically overrides the `path` variable on line 270 if it is not null. This matches the exact root cause of the vulnerability. There is no sanitization or restriction on this parameter, meaning the vulnerability is present and not fixed.
